tech.title: First SMS (text message) ever sent
tech.description: Engineer Neil Papworth sends the world’s first SMS message reading 'Merry Christmas' from a computer to a mobile phone on the Vodafone network.

tech.title: XBox 360 releases in Europe
tech.description: Microsoft officially launches the Xbox 360 console in Europe, accelerating the global console wars between Sony and Nintendo.

tech.title: Bhopal disaster leaks begin triggering global safety reforms
tech.description: Although the major gas leak occurred on December 2–3, the unfolding crisis on this day led to major changes in chemical safety regulations worldwide.

history.title: Neon lighting demonstrated for the first time
history.description: French inventor Georges Claude publicly displays the first neon lighting tubes at the Paris Motor Show, leading to decades of neon signage across the world.

history.title: First successful human heart transplant
history.description: Dr. Christiaan Barnard performs the world’s first successful human heart transplant in Cape Town, South Africa.

history.title: George H. W. Bush and Mikhail Gorbachev declare the Cold War over
history.description: At the Malta Summit, the leaders of the United States and Soviet Union announce that the decades-long Cold War is effectively ending.

world_events.title: Pakistan civil war intensifies as India enters the conflict
world_events.description: India formally joins the Bangladesh Liberation War, leading to a rapid escalation and eventual independence of Bangladesh.

world_events.title: Union Carbide gas leak kills thousands in continuing Bhopal disaster
world_events.description: The effects of the toxic methyl isocyanate gas leak continue into December 3, making it one of history’s worst industrial catastrophes.

world_events.title: Iconic Dubai Frame skyscraper's structural completion
world_events.description: Dubai completes the structure of the Dubai Frame, which later becomes one of the city's most recognizable architectural landmarks.

world_events.title: New York grand jury declines to indict officer in Eric Garner case
world_events.description: A major national controversy erupts in the United States after a grand jury decides not to indict NYPD officer Daniel Pantaleo over the death of Eric Garner.

births.title: Birth of Joseph Conrad
births.description: Polish-British writer best known for 'Heart of Darkness' and other works exploring morality and colonialism.

births.title: Birth of Julianne Moore
births.description: American actress known for her roles in drama and independent films, including 'Still Alice' and 'Boogie Nights.'

births.title: Birth of Amanda Seyfried
births.description: American actress and singer known for films such as 'Mamma Mia!', 'Mean Girls', and 'Les Misérables.'

deaths.title: Death of Pierre-Auguste Renoir
deaths.description: French painter and leading Impressionist known for his vibrant light and saturated color.

deaths.title: Death of Scatman John
deaths.description: American musician known for his unique blend of scat singing and dance music, including his hit 'Scatman (Ski-Ba-Bop-Ba-Dop-Bop)'.

deaths.title: Death of Shashi Kapoor
deaths.description: Legendary Indian actor and filmmaker who starred in more than 150 films and became a major figure in Indian cinema.
